author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Luke xii. 15, ‘And he said unto them, take heed, and keep yourselves from all covetousness.’

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Luke xii. 15, ‘And he said unto them, take heed, and keep yourselves from all covetousness.’

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I doubt not but that ye who fear God, do hear His word with awe, and execute it with cheerfulness; that what He hath promised, ye may at present hope for, hereafter receive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have just now heard the Lord Christ Jesus, the Son of God, giving us a precept.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Truth, who neither deceiveth, nor is deceived, hath given us a precept; let us hear, fear, beware.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is this precept then: “I say unto you, Beware of all covetousness”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “of all covetousness”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “of all”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why did He add, “of all”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He might have spoken thus, “Beware of covetousness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It suited Him to add, “of all;” and to say, “Beware of all covetousness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why He said this, the occasion as it were out of which these words arose, is shown to us in the holy Gospel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A certain man appealed to Him against his brother, who had taken away all his patrimony, and gave not back his proper portion to his brother.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye see then how good a case this appellant had.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he was not seeking to take by violence another’s, but was seeking only for his own which had been left him by his parents; these was he demanding back by his appeal to the judgment of the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He had an unrighteous brother; but against an unrighteous brother had he found a righteous Judge.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ought he then in so good a cause to lose that opportunity?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or who would say to his brother, “Restore to thy brother his portion,” if Christ would not say it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Would that judge be likely to say it, whom perhaps his richer and extortionate brother might corrupt by a bribe?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Forlorn then as he was, and despoiled of his father’s goods, when he had found such and so great a Judge he goes up to Him, he appeals to, he beseeches Him, he lays his cause before Him in few words.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what occasion was there to set forth his cause at length, when he was speaking to Him who could even see the heart?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Master,” he says, “speak to my brother, that he divide the inheritance with me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord did not say to him, “Let thy brother come.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No, He neither sent for him to be present, nor in his presence did He say to him who had appealed to Him, “Prove what thou wast saying.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He asked for half an inheritance, he asked for half an inheritance on earth; the Lord offered him a whole inheritance in heaven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord gave more than asked for.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Speak to my brother, that he divide the inheritance with me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Just case, short case.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But let us hear Him who at once gives judgment and instruction.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Man,” He saith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “O man;” for seeing thou valuest this inheritance so highly, what art thou but a man?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He wished to make him something more than man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What more did He wish to make him, from whom He wished to take covetousness away?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What more did He wish to make him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will tell you, “I have said, Ye are gods, and all of you are children of the Most High.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, what He wished to make him, to reckon him that hath no covetousness among the “gods.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Man, who made Me a divider among you?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So the Apostle Paul His servant, when he said, “I beseech you, brethren, that ye all speak the same thing, and that there be no schisms among you,” was unwilling to be a divider.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And afterwards he thus admonished them who were running after his name, and dividing Christ: “Every one of you saith, I am of Paul, and I of Apollos, and I of Cephas, and I of Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is Christ divided?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Was Paul crucified for you? or were ye baptized in the name of Paul?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Judge then, how wicked are those men, who would have Him to be divided, who would not be a divider.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Who,” saith He, “hath made Me a divider among you?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast petitioned for a kindness; hear counsel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I say unto you, Beware of all covetousness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Perhaps,” he would say, “thou wouldest call him covetous and greedy, if he were seeking another’s goods; but I say, seek not even thine own greedily or covetously.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is “Of all, beware of all covetousness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A heavy burden this!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If by any chance this burden be imposed on them that are weak; let Him be sought unto, that He who imposes it, may vouchsafe to give us strength.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is not a thing to be lightly regarded, my Brethren, when our Lord, our Redeemer, our Saviour, who died for us, who gave His Own Blood as our ransom, to redeem us, our Advocate and Judge; it is no light matter when He saith, “Beware.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He knoweth well how great the evil is; we know it not, let us believe Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Beware,” saith He.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore? of what? “of all covetousness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am but keeping what is mine own, I am not taking away another’s; “Beware of all covetousness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not only is he covetous, who plunders the goods of others; but he is covetous too, who greedily keeps his own.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if he is so blamed who greedily keeps his own; how is he condemned who plunders what is another’s!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Beware,” He saith, “of all covetousness: For a man’s life consisteth not in the abundance of the things which he possesseth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He that stores up great abundance, how much does he take therefrom to live?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When he has taken it, and in a way separated in thought sufficient to live upon from it, let him consider for whom the rest remains; lest haply when thou keepest wherewith to live, thou art gathering only wherewith to die.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Behold Christ, behold truth, behold severity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Beware,” saith truth: “Beware,” saith severity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou love not the truth, fear severity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “A man’s life consisteth not in the abundance of the things which he possesseth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Believe Him, He doth not deceive thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: On the other hand, thou sayest, “Yea, ‘a man’s life’ does ‘consist in the abundance of the things which he possesses.’”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He doth not deceive thee; thou deceivest thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Out of this occasion then, when that appellant was seeking his own portion, not desiring to plunder another’s, arose that sentence of the Lord, wherein He said not, “Beware of covetousness;” but added, “of all covetousness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nor was this all: He giveth another example of a certain rich man, “whose ground had turned out well.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “There was,” He saith, “a certain rich man, whose ground had turned out well.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “had turned out well”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The ground which he possessed had brought forth a great produce.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So that he could not find where to bestow it: suddenly, through his abundance he became straitened—this old covetous man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For how many years had already passed away, and yet those barns had been enough?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So great then was the produce, that the accustomed places were not sufficient.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the wretched man sought counsel, not as to how he should lay the additional produce out, but how he should store it up; and in thinking he discovered an expedient.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He seemed as it were wise in his own eyes, by the discovery of this expedient.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Knowingly did he think of it, wisely hit upon it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What was this he wisely hit upon?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I will destroy,” he says, “my” old “barns, and will build new ones greater, and will fill them; and I will say to my soul.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What wilt thou say to thy soul?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Soul, thou hast much goods laid up for many years, take thine ease, eat, drink, be merry.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This did the wise discoverer of this expedient say to his soul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And God,” who doth not disdain to speak even with fools, “said unto him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Some of you may peradventure say, And how did God speak with a fool?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O, my Brethren, with how many fools does He speak here, when the Gospel is read!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When it is read, are not they who hear and do not, fools?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then did the Lord say?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he, I repeat, thought himself wise by the discovery of his expedient.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thou fool,” He saith; “Thou fool,” who seemest wise unto thyself; “Thou fool,” who hast said to thy soul, “Thou hast much goods laid up for many years: to-day is thy soul required of thee!”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thy soul to which thou hast said, “Thou hast much goods,” to-day is “required,” and hath no good at all.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let it then despise these goods, and be herself good, that when she is “required,” she may depart in assured hope.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what is more perverse than a man who wishes to have “much goods,” and does not wish to be good himself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Unworthy art thou to have them, who dost not wish to be what thou dost wish to have.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For dost thou wish to have a bad country house?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No indeed, but a good one.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or a bad wife?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No, but a good one.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or a bad hood?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or even a bad shoe?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And why a bad soul only?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He did not in this place say to this fool who was thinking on vain things, building barns, and who had no regard to the wants of the poor; He did not say to him, “To-day shall thy soul be hurried away to hell:” He said no such thing as this, but “is required of thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I do not tell thee whither thy soul shall go; yet hence, where thou art laying up for it such store of things, must it depart, whether thou wilt or no.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, “thou fool,” thou hast thought to fill thy new and greater barns, as if there was nothing to be done with what thou hast.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But peradventure he was not yet a Christian.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us hear then, Brethren, to whom as believers the Gospel is read, by whom He who spake these things, is worshipped, whose mark is borne by us on our forehead, and is held in the heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For of very great concernment is it where a man hath the mark of Christ, whether in the forehead, or both in the forehead and the heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye have heard to-day the words of the holy prophet Ezekiel, how that before God sent one to destroy the ungodly people, He first sent one to mark them, and said to him, “Go and set a mark upon the foreheads of the men that sigh and moan for the sins of my people that are done in the midst of them.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He did not say, “which are done without them;” but “in the midst of them.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet they “sigh and moan;” and therefore are they “marked on the forehead:” in the forehead of the inner man, not the outer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For there is a forehead in the face, there is a forehead in the conscience.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So it happens that when the inner forehead is stricken, the outer grows red; either red with shame, or pale with fear.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then there is a forehead of the inner man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There were they “marked” that they might not be destroyed; because though they did not correct the sins which were “done in the midst of them,” yet they sorrowed for them, and by that very sorrow separated themselves; and though separated in God’s sight, they were mixed with them in the eyes of men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are “marked” secretly, are not hurt openly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Afterwards the Destroyer is sent, and to him it is said, “Go, lay waste, spare neither young nor old, male nor female, but come not near those who have the mark on their forehead.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How great security is granted to you, my Brethren, who among this people are sighing, and moaning for the iniquities which are being done in the midst of you, and who do them not!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But that ye may not commit iniquities, “beware of all covetousness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will tell you in its full extent, what is “of all covetousness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In matter of lust he is covetous, whom his own wife suffices not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And idolatry itself is called covetousness; because again in matter of divine worship he is covetous, whom the one and true God suffices not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What but the covetous soul makes for itself many gods?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What but the covetous soul makes to itself false martyrs?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Beware of all covetousness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, thou lovest thine own goods, and dost boast thyself in that thou seekest not the goods of others; see what evil thou doest in not hearing Christ, who saith, “Beware of all covetousness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See thou dost love thine own goods, thou dost not take away the goods of others; thou hast the fruits of thy labour, they are justly thine; thou hast been left an heir, some one whose good graces thou hast attained has given it to thee; thou hast been on the sea, and in its perils, hast committed no fraud, hast sworn no lie, hast acquired what it hath pleased God thou shouldest; and thou art keeping it greedily as in a good conscience, because thou dost not possess it from evil sources, and dost not seek what is another’s.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet if thou give not heed to Him who hath said, “Beware of all covetousness,” hear how great evils thou wilt be ready to do for thine own goods’ sake.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, for example, it hath chanced to thee to be made a judge.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wilt not be corrupted, because thou dost not seek the goods of others; no one giveth thee a bribe and says, “Give judgment against my adversary.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This be far from thee, a man, who seekest not the things of others, how couldest thou be persuaded to do this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet see what evil thou wilt be ready to do for thine own goods’ sake.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Peradventure he that wishes thee to judge evilly, and pronounce sentence for him against his adversary is a powerful man, and able to bring up false accusation against thee, that thou mayest lose what thou hast.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou dost reflect, and think upon his power, think of thine own goods thou art keeping, which thou dost love: not which thou hast possessed, but in whose power rather thou art thyself unhappily fixed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This thy bird-lime, by reason of which thou hast not the wings of virtue free, thou dost look to; and thou sayest within thine own self, “I am offending this man, he has much influence in the world; he will suggest evil accusations against me, and I shall be outlawed, and lose all I have.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus thou wilt give unrighteous judgment, not when thou seekest another’s, but when thou keepest thine own.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give me a man who has given ear to Christ, give me a man who has heard with fear “Beware of all covetousness;” and let him not say to me, “I am a poor man, a plebeian of mean estate, one of the common people, how can I hope ever to be a judge?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am in no fear of this temptation, the peril of which thou hast placed before mine eyes.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet lo, even this poor man I will tell what he ought to fear.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Some rich and powerful person calls thee to give false witness for him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What wilt thou be doing now?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast a good little property of thine own; thou hast laboured for it, hast acquired, and kept it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That person requires of thee; “Give false witness for me, and I will give thee so and so much.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou who seekest not the things of others, sayest, “That be far from me: I do not seek for what it has not pleased God to give me, I will not receive it; depart from me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Hast thou no wish to receive what I give?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will take away what thou hast already.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See now prove thyself, question now thine own self.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why dost thou look at me?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Look inward on thine own self, look at thine own self within, examine thine own self within; sit down before thine own self, and summon thine own self before thee, and stretch thyself upon the rack of God’s commandment, and torment thyself with His fear, and deal not softly with thyself; answer thine own self.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, if any one were to threaten thee with this, what wouldest thou do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I will take away from thee what with so great labour thou hast acquired, if thou wilt not give false witness for me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give him that; “Beware of all covetousness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “O my servant,” He will say to thee, “whom I have redeemed and made free, whom from a servant I have adopted to be a brother, whom I have set as a member in My Body, give ear to Me: He may take away what thou hast acquired, Me he shall not take away from thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Art thou keeping thine own goods, that thou mayest not perish?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What, have I not said unto thee, ‘Beware of all covetousness’?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, thou art in confusion, tossed to and fro; thy heart as a ship is shaken about by tempests.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ is asleep: awake Him, that sleepeth, and thou shalt be exposed no more to the raging of the storm.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Awake Him, who was pleased to have nothing here, and thou hast all, who came even to the Cross for thee, whose “Bones” as He was naked and hanging “were numbered” by them that mocked Him; and “beware of all covetousness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Covetousness of money is not all; “beware of covetousness” of life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A dreadful covetousness, covetousness much to be feared.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Sometimes a man will despise what he has, and say, “I will not give false witness; I will not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You tell me, I will take away what thou hast.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Take away what I have; you do not take away what I have within.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he was not left a poor man, who said, ‘The Lord gave, the Lord hath taken away; it is done as it pleased the Lord; blessed’ therefore ‘be the Name of the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Naked came I out of my mother’s womb, naked shall I return to the earth.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Naked outwardly, well-clothed within.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Naked as regards these rags, these corruptible rags outwardly, clothed within.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: ‘Let thy priests be clothed with righteousness.’”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what if he say to thee, when thou hast despised the things which thou possessest, what if he say to thee, “I will kill thee”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou have given ear to Christ, answer him, “Wilt Thou kill me?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Better that thou shouldest kill my body, than that I by a false tongue should kill my soul!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What canst thou do to me?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wilt kill my body; my soul will depart at liberty, to receive again at the end of the world even this very body she hath despised.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What canst thou do to me then?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whereas if I should give false witness for thee, with thy tongue do I kill myself; and not in my body do I kill myself; ‘For the mouth that lieth killeth the soul.’”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But peradventure thou dost not say so.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And why dost thou not say so?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wishest to live; thou wishest to live longer than God hath appointed for thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Dost thou then “beware of all covetousness”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So long was it God’s will that thou shouldest live, till this person came to thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It may be that he will kill thee, to make a martyr of thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Entertain then no undue desire of life; and so thou wilt not have an eternity of death.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye see how that covetousness everywhere, when we wish for more than is necessary, causes us to sin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Beware we of all covetousness, if we would enjoy eternal wisdom.
